title: caltha laeta subsp. rostrata
intro: Caltha laeta subsp. rostrata, also known as marsh marigold, is a flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is valued for its potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting liver function. The roots and rhizomes of the plant can be used to prepare herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions. However, it is essential to note that the plant contains toxic compounds called glycosides, which can be hazardous if ingested in large quantities or by individuals with certain health conditions.
